a-c-k.bio: Alpha Centauri Kid - a.c.k. - is an artist and musician whose work spans glitch animation, digital painting, and textured physicals, unified by three recurring motifs: flowers, pianos, and the Muse, the creative force he describes himself as serving.

beeple.bio: Beeple - Mike Winkelmann - has posted a new artwork every day since 2007, a discipline that became Everydays: The First 5000 Days, whose 2021 Christie's sale brought digital art into the auction mainstream. His practice spans those daily sketches, kinetic sculpture, and pointed cultural commentary.

dmitri-cherniak.bio: Dmitri Cherniak's practice in long-form generative art pays homage to 20th-century movements through algorithmic reinterpretation. Ringers - one thousand algorithmic studies of a string wrapped around pegs - is its defining series.

kim-asendorf.bio: Kim Asendorf has been working with code since the net art era - his open-source pixel sorting algorithm, released in 2010, became one of glitch culture's foundational tools. His digital art practice spans real-time generative systems and interactive works like PXL DEX.

larva-labs.bio: Larva Labs - Matt Hall and John Watkinson - created CryptoPunks in 2017: ten thousand algorithmically generated characters, claimed for free, that became the archetype for on-chain identity, helping pioneer on-chain generative art and sparking the NFT movement.

operator.bio: Operator - Ania Catherine and Dejha Ti - pair choreography with code, building conceptual systems that join environments, technology, and the body. Human Unreadable, winner of the 2023 Lumen Prize for generative art, derives each work from an on-chain choreographic sequence, released in stages from abstraction to live performance.

refik-anadol.bio: Refik Anadol uses machine learning to render data as moving image. Unsupervised was the first NFT acquired into MoMA's permanent collection. Winds of Yawanawa and Biome Lumina extended the practice into living, dynamic works - the throughline that led to Dataland, his Museum of AI Arts in Los Angeles.

sam-spratt.bio: Sam Spratt brings old-world craftsmanship to digital tools. Through Luci, he has built a sustained narrative world across six chapters, treating blockchain as a medium for myth-making and community participation.

tyler-hobbs-and-dandelion-wist.bio: A collaboration between Tyler Hobbs and Dandelion Wist, QQL is a generative art algorithm that invites collectors to compose their own outputs - blending the artist’s system with the collector’s creative input.

tyler-hobbs.bio: Tyler Hobbs is a leading voice in generative art, defined by his 2021 collection Fidenza. His practice pairs precise algorithmic design with the chaos that emerges from human input and computational surprise.

xcopy.bio: XCOPY's glitch aesthetic explores death, dystopia, and apathy - a raw critique of capitalism and technology. His work confronts viewers with scenes of exploitation, decay, and digital-era disillusionment.

cryptopunks.curatorNote:
CryptoPunks established the cultural foundation for everything that followed in NFTs - at once a historical artefact and an on-chain identity standard.

Hivemind's curation focuses on a concentration of core traits - Big Beard, Hoodie, Cap Forward, Tiara - supported by a selection of clean Punks with premium characteristics.

dataland-biome-lumina.curatorNote:
Biome Lumina opens a new register for AI-driven art: works that breathe and respond rather than resolve as static outputs. As one of the founding works of Anadol's Dataland, it helps open the first institution dedicated to AI art.

Hivemind's eighteen pieces span twelve species clusters across flora and fungi, including a Rare Mystical Variant.

day-gardens.curatorNote:
Day Gardens shows Hobbs returning to the painterly with the discipline of his algorithmic practice intact - gestural, observational works that pair systematic process with the hand.

Hivemind's triptych spans the series' tonal range, from restrained to expressive.

fidenza.curatorNote:
Fidenza is the definitive Art Blocks work and a cornerstone of the digital art canon - 999 flow-field compositions that set the benchmark for long-form on-chain generative art.

Hivemind's curation is built on a selection of core traits which beautifully represent the capability and variety of Hobbs' algorithm, including White Mono, Spiral, Black & White and Micro.

grifters.curatorNote:
Grifters is XCOPY's preeminent PFP collection - a generative cast of 666 characters in his cynical visual language.

Hivemind has collected color sets for Wretch, Shady and G to the M Grifters, anchored by one of three Legendary outputs: ROTTEN. Grifters gonna grift!

harbor-scene.curatorNote:
Harbor Scene is a contemporary response to American Impressionism: three unique iterations generated in response to John Henry Twachtman's unfinished Harbor Scene, housed in LACMA's collection.

Hivemind acquired Harbor Scene #2 from LACMA and Cactoid Labs' Remembrance of Things Future project to complement an already expansive Hobbs 1/1 collection.

her-favorite-flowers.curatorNote:
Her favorite flowers are a January 2023 study in textured blossom work - the seed of what has defined a.c.k.'s recent practice.

kissed by the Moonlight continues the a.c.k. blossom arc for Hivemind and is a visually and thematically perfect 1/1 to sit alongside our Piano Blossoms set.

human-unreadable.curatorNote:
Human Unreadable merges dance, code, photography, and performance into expressive figures shaped by code. The series defined what it means to own and preserve performance.

Hivemind holds eighteen generative works and the only large-scale Human Unreadable X-Ray Machine sculpture.

lights.curatorNote:
Lights is a collection of 1/1 real-time animations running on a custom smart contract written for on-chain artworks, through which the code can be delivered without any third party server involvement.

Hivemind holds Raster und Spektrum; a unique, deterministic, fully on-chain real-time animation, written in WebGL2 and JavaScript with no external dependencies. It was unveiled at Art Basel Miami 2025.

lightyears.curatorNote:
Light Years is a Bauhaus-informed exploration of color and form, created in collaboration with the Estate of László Moholy-Nagy, the pioneering Hungarian photographer. It connects Cherniak's algorithmic practice with 20th-century modernism.

Hivemind holds six Light Years, capturing our interest in how technology and cutting edge materials have long informed artistic practices.

masks-of-luci.curatorNote:
Masks of Luci is a collection of 613 hand-drawn and generative artworks, each carrying imagery related to collector-authored language and ritualized meaning. It's the most recent installment in Spratt's Luci series.

Hivemind owns 2 full sets of Masks - each of the 8 Categories - alongside 2 Council Masks, giving us a firm holding in the latest chapter.

piano-blossoms.curatorNote:
The Piano Blossoms auction marked a pivotal moment in a.c.k.'s career - five 1/1 digital paintings with physical counterparts, each placing his recurring motifs of piano and blossom into a different scene from cultural memory spanning literature, painting and cinema. The physical counterparts were printed at Make-Ready in 31 layers of UV-raised pigmentation.

Hivemind holds the complete suite, presented at a.c.k.'s first solo exhibition - in Amsterdam in October 2024 with Avant Arte - sharing the contract with the artist's post-auction self-portrait.

pxl-dex.curatorNote:
PXL DEX is part of Kim Asendorf's systemic investigation of the pixel as a primary unit of digital expression. Each piece's pixel allowance is a finite, depletable resource - precise and rooted in the language of computation.

Hivemind holds five PXL DEX, with each pixel contributing to a unique artistic and economic system.

pxl-pod.curatorNote: PXL POD continues Asendorf's pixel-native practice into modular composition, where each piece reads as both discrete artwork and node in a larger formal grammar.

qql.curatorNote: QQL turned generative art into a participatory medium: collectors compose their own outputs from Hobbs and Wist's algorithm. Hivemind holds two minted compositions alongside ten unspent Mint Passes - a position on both authorship and reserve.

repeat-as-necessary.curatorNote: Operator's continued investigation of the body as algorithmic input. The four-piece sequence forms a meditative arc around breath, presence, and dissolution.

ringers.curatorNote: Ringers is the algorithmic counterpart to Fidenza - proof that constraint produces inexhaustible variety. Hivemind's 36-piece holding is among the largest single-collector positions in the project, organized to read as a coherent visual taxonomy.

skulls-of-luci.curatorNote: The precursor to Masks of Luci - Sam Spratt's earlier exploration of the same formal language with rawer, more elemental material. Historical anchors for the larger Masks practice.

superrare-beeple.curatorNote: Beeple's TIME cover collaboration is a watershed in the cultural legitimization of digital art - the moment a major legacy publication recognized crypto-native artists as their visual peers.

superrare-dmitri-cherniak.curatorNote: Hivemind holds two of the four algorithmic editions of A Slight Lack of Symmetry Can Cause So Much Pain, Cherniak's earliest SuperRare series, which extends his Art Blocks practice into the 1/1 register.
superrare-dmitri-cherniak.essayTitle: Into the Collection: A Slight Lack of Symmetry

superrare-xcopy.curatorNote: Hivemind's XCOPY 1/1s span the artist's most-cited cultural moments - works that helped define crypto art's visual register: glitch, dread, and political commentary at compressed scale.

synthetic-dreams.curatorNote: Created for the Google Quantum Summer Symposium 2021, each piece is generated from quantum random states. The work documents an early collaboration between a major artist and bleeding-edge computational research.

tyler-hobbs.curatorNote: Hobbs' personal 1/1 practice - Return Zero, Elektroanima, One One Overflow - represents his most intentional standalone statements outside the generative editions. Each piece refines a different dimension of his algorithmic vocabulary.

winds-of-yawanawa.curatorNote: Anadol's data-painting practice paired with the Yawanawa community of the Brazilian Amazon. The project channels collector capital toward indigenous cultural preservation.
winds-of-yawanawa.essayTitle: Inside the Collection: Winds of Yawanawa
